content.full_text:
Letter from Chris Chandler to Fair Political Practices Commission

Date: October 10, 1992

Recipient:
Fair Political Practices Commission
Mr. Ben Davidian, Chairman
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Sender:
Chris Chandler
Assemblyman, Third District

Content:

Dear Chairman Davidian:

As my tenure in the State Assembly draws to a close, there are several questions that I would like to raise regarding the disposition of property and assets held by my campaign committee, Friends of Chris Chandler. Also, I would like to know the procedure by which I would create a new committee, using any remaining assets held by the committee being terminated.

In specific, since it's creation, the committee has acquired equipment such as: car phones, computer, laser printer, television and video cassette recorder, fax machine. I would like the correct procedure for the sale and reporting of these and other similar items.

In addition, I require information regarding the regulations dealing with which financial materials must be retained and for what amount of time. These would include prior FPPC filings and banking materials.

Your written response to this letter is greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
CHRIS CHANDLER
